workflow.name: Analyze contract risk from PDFs with OpenAI, Supabase and Slack alerts
workflow.description:
## Who it's for

Legal, Procurement, and Compliance teams at mid-size companies. ESN and agencies selling AI-powered contract review as a service.

## How it works

Upload a contract PDF through a rich form (with metadata: contract type, counterparty, department, submitter). The workflow first checks Supabase for duplicates to avoid re-analyzing the same contract. Then it runs a **two-pass AI analysis**: Pass 1 classifies the contract (type, parties, dates, jurisdiction), Pass 2 performs deep risk analysis (clause-by-clause risk levels, missing clauses, obligations, negotiation points, compliance flags). Results are scored 0–100 with risk levels (CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW). A structured report is generated as both **Slack Block Kit** (rich formatting) and **HTML email**. High-risk contracts trigger a Slack alert with full blocks and an email report to the submitter. Low-risk contracts get a summary Slack notification. All analyses are stored in Supabase with full metadata for audit. An Error Trigger sends admin notifications to a dedicated Slack channel.

## How to set up

1. Run the SQL from the "Supabase Schema" sticky note in Supabase SQL Editor.
2. Set environment variables: `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY`.
3. Add credentials in n8n: **OpenAI** (Header Auth), **Supabase**, **Slack**, **Gmail** (optional).
4. Configure the **Config** node: RISK_THRESHOLD, SLACK_CHANNEL, ADMIN_SLACK_CHANNEL, AI_MODEL, ALERT_EMAIL, ENABLE_EMAIL, CONTRACT_LANG.
5. Activate the workflow and share the Form URL with your team.

parameters.content:
## Enterprise AI Contract Intelligence - Pro

**Who it's for:** Legal, Procurement, Compliance teams. ESN/agencies.

**What it does:**
1. Upload contract PDF via form (with metadata: type, department, counterparty)
2. Deduplication check against Supabase
3. AI Pass 1 - Classification (parties, dates, jurisdiction, contract type)
4. AI Pass 2 - Deep risk analysis (clauses, red flags, obligations, recommendations)
5. Build structured report (Slack Blocks + HTML email)
6. Store in Supabase with full metadata
7. Slack Block Kit alert (high risk) or summary (low risk)
8. Gmail HTML report to submitter (configurable)
9. Error handling with admin Slack channel

**Setup:** Run the SQL in the "Supabase Schema" sticky. Add credentials: OpenAI (Header Auth), Supabase, Slack, Gmail. Configure the Config node.

**Supabase Schema** — Run in Supabase SQL Editor before use:

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS contract_analyses (
  id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
  filename TEXT NOT NULL,
  contract_type TEXT,
  jurisdiction TEXT,
  submitter_name TEXT,
  submitter_email TEXT,
  department TEXT,
  counterparty TEXT,
  raw_text_preview TEXT,
  parties JSONB,
  key_clauses JSONB,
  overall_risk_score INT DEFAULT 0,
  risk_level TEXT DEFAULT 'unknown',
  executive_summary TEXT,
  top_risks JSONB,
  recommendations JSONB,
  classification JSONB,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'analyzed'
    CHECK (status IN ('analyzed','flagged','archived','reviewing')),
  analyzed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ca_score
  ON contract_analyses(overall_risk_score DESC);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ca_status
  ON contract_analyses(status);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ca_type
  ON contract_analyses(contract_type);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ca_created
  ON contract_analyses(created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ca_filename
  ON contract_analyses(filename);
```

**Step 1 - Ingest**

Form upload with metadata (contract type, submitter, department, counterparty) or Manual test with sample data. Config node centralizes all settings.

**Step 2 - Deduplicate**

Query Supabase for existing analysis with the same filename. If found, skip analysis and show previous result. Avoids wasting AI tokens on re-uploads.

**Step 3 - Extract & Prepare**

Extract text from PDF binary, normalize, truncate to 12k chars, merge with form metadata and config for downstream AI calls.

**Step 4 - AI Two-Pass Analysis**

Pass 1: Quick classification (type, parties, dates, jurisdiction, ~300 tokens).
Pass 2: Deep risk analysis (clauses, obligations, red flags, recommendations, ~2000 tokens).
Split for cost efficiency and structured output.

**Step 5 - Report, Store & Alert**

Build Report generates Slack Block Kit JSON + HTML email body.
Supabase insert stores full analysis with metadata.
High risk → Slack Blocks alert + Gmail HTML report.
Low risk → Slack summary notification.
Form Ending shows completion to user.

**Error Handling**

Error Trigger catches any workflow failure and sends a notification to the admin Slack channel (#n8n-errors). Includes workflow name, error message, and timestamp.

parameters.formDescription: Upload a contract PDF for AI-powered analysis. The system will extract key clauses, assess risk, and provide actionable recommendations.

parameters.completionMessage: This contract has already been analyzed. Check Supabase for the existing report.

assignments.name: contractText
assignments.value:
SAMPLE CONTRACT FOR TESTING

This Master Service Agreement (MSA) is entered into as of January 15, 2025, by and between:

Party A: TechCorp International Ltd., a company incorporated under the laws of France, with registered office at 42 Avenue des Champs-Élysées, 75008 Paris ("Client")

Party B: CloudVendor Solutions Inc., a Delaware corporation, with principal office at 100 Market Street, San Francisco, CA 94105 ("Vendor")

1. TERM: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of thirty-six (36) months, automatically renewing for successive 12-month periods unless terminated.

2. TERMINATION: Either party may terminate with 30 days written notice. Client may terminate for convenience with 15 days notice. Early termination fee: 50% of remaining contract value.

3. LIABILITY: Vendor's total aggregate liability shall not exceed the fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim. EXCLUSION: This cap does NOT apply to breaches of confidentiality, IP infringement, or gross negligence.

4. INDEMNIFICATION: Vendor shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client against all third-party claims arising from: (a) IP infringement, (b) data breaches caused by Vendor negligence, (c) violation of applicable law. Client indemnifies Vendor against claims arising from Client's misuse of services.

5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY: All pre-existing IP remains with original owner. Work product created specifically for Client shall be owned by Client upon full payment. Vendor retains license to use general methodologies and tools.

6. CONFIDENTIALITY: 5-year obligation post-termination. Covers all non-public business and technical information. Standard exceptions apply (public domain, independent development, legal compulsion).

7. DATA PROTECTION: Vendor shall comply with GDPR and applicable data protection laws. DPA attached as Annex B. Sub-processors require prior written consent.

8. WARRANTY: Vendor warrants services will be performed in a professional manner consistent with industry standards. 30-day cure period for material defects.

9. GOVERNING LAW: This Agreement shall be governed by French law. Disputes shall be resolved by the Commercial Court of Paris.

10. FORCE MAJEURE: Neither party liable for delays due to events beyond reasonable control, provided affected party notifies within 5 business days.

parameters.completionMessage:
={{ 'Contract: ' + ($json.filename ?? 'N/A') + '
Risk Score: ' + ($json.overall_risk_score ?? 'N/A') + '/100 (' + ($json.risk_level ?? 'N/A') + ')
Type: ' + ($json.classification?.contract_type ?? $json.contract_type ?? 'N/A') + '

' + ($json.executive_summary ?? '') + '

Full report stored in Supabase.' + ($json.ENABLE_EMAIL ? ' Email report sent to ' + ($json.submitter_email || $json.ALERT_EMAIL || '') + '.' : '') }}

data.details: In n8n, when a workflow execution fails, it can start another workflow. This second workflow can be any arbitrary workflow on your n8n instance. Use the Error Trigger node as your Trigger in the Error workflow.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
